articles.title: Gunman in Shooting Near White House Killed in Exchange of Fire With Secret Service
articles.summary: A gunman known to the U.S. Secret Service opened fire near the White House and was shot and killed by federal officers. President Trump was in the White House at the time.

articles.title: Trump Is Setting His Sights on Restricting Legal Immigration
articles.summary: After a year focused on immigrants here illegally, the Trump administration is now making it harder for legal migrants to stay in the country. It is a risky pivot.

articles.title: Iran Agreed to Give Up Enriched Uranium in Deal Announced by Trump, U.S. Officials Say
articles.summary: Precise details were unclear but the officials said the U.S. demanded a commitment from Iran on uranium as part of any initial agreement.

articles.title: Secret Service fatally shoots suspect outside White House checkpoint, bystander wounded
articles.summary: The suspect began shooting at posted officers, who returned fire and hit the shooter, the Secret Service said. A bystander was also shot, a law enforcement official said, but it's unclear by whom.

articles.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
articles.summary: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

articles.title: Trump says a deal with Iran and opening of Strait of Hormuz are 'largely negotiated'
articles.summary: President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. It aims to end the war and includes two-month negotiations on Iran's nuclear program.

articles.title: Garden Grove Chemical Tank Could Be Inching Toward Explosion, Authorities Say
articles.summary: Firefighters still have few options to prevent a tank of toxic chemicals in Garden Grove, Calif., from leaking or exploding.

articles.title: The squeeze on Cuba now includes compensation lawsuits
articles.summary: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in conte

articles.title: Severe global food crisis could come within the year, says UN agency
articles.summary: Maximo Torero, Chief Economist at The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, warns that the war in Iran is choking the global supply of fertilizer and a food crisis could follow with

articles.title: Video shows Israeli 'double-tap strike' killed medics and a toddler in Lebanon
articles.summary: In Lebanon, an Israeli 'double tap' killed three medics and four others including a two-year-old girl. A neighbor's video shows what happened.

articles.title: Trump Administration Chips Away at Last Traces of Broad Inquiry Into Jan. 6
articles.summary: Two recent moves aligned the department ever more closely with President Trump’s own efforts to whitewash the events of that day.

articles.title: What will Trump do next with Iran?
articles.summary: Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.

articles.title: Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, his family says
articles.summary: The two-time NASCAR champion died on Thursday, a day after passing out in a Chevrolet simulator.

articles.title: A little bit country, a little bit Desi: A Pakistani-American's hybrid music
articles.summary: On May 31, Mo Sabri makes a landmark appearance with the Nashville Symphony for an orchestral rendition of his new album, "Tennessee Desi," which blends Appalachian styles with Muslim devotional music

articles.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
articles.summary: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

articles.title: Opinion: Remembering Barney Frank, trailblazing public servant
articles.summary: Mass. congressman Barney Frank was the first House member to come out as gay and was instrumental in Wall Street reforms after the Great Recession. He died this week at the age of 86.

articles.title: NATO allies confused by Trump's unexpected move to deploy 5,000 U.S. troops to Poland
articles.summary: Some NATO allies say they are confused by President Trump's different statements on U.S. troop deployments to Europe.

articles.title: Nicolas Cage Made Himself a Legend. Then He Had to Live With It.
articles.summary: The iconic actor on becoming a meme, a myth and larger than life.

articles.title: Short Naps, Long Hours: How Autism Clinics Squeeze Medicaid Dollars Out of Preschoolers
articles.summary: The industry has grown rapidly, straining state budgets. A focus on finances has led to overbilling, fraud and even harm.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
